commit.message:
fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

fix: intelligent duplicate detection with subject normalization and task reactivation

Problem: Scan created duplicate tasks for the same email thread when
subject prefixes differed (e.g. 'Re: RG Netzwerkprüfung' vs
'[EXTERN] RG Netzwerkprüfung'). Done tasks were only skipped, never
reactivated when new activity appeared.

Changes:
- Add stripSubjectPrefixes() to remove Re:, Fw:, AW:, WG:, [EXTERN],
  [EXTERNAL] etc. before comparison
- normalizeForCompare() now preserves German umlauts (äöüß) and strips
  prefixes before lowercasing
- isSimilarTitle() uses dual threshold: Jaccard >0.6 OR subset ratio
  >=0.9 (catches prefix-only differences)
- Safety-net now reactivates done tasks instead of creating duplicates:
  sets status to needs-attention, adds reactivation history entry,
  resets enrichment for fresh content extraction
- Backward-compat dedup uses normalized comparison instead of exact match

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

commit.message:
fix: re-render detail panel after unfreezeTask to reflect changes

unfreezeTask() previously only removed the frozen CSS class but did not
re-render the task card or detail panel. This caused a bug where
immediate intents (update/summarize/rename/answer) processed the data
correctly on the server but the UI never showed the changes — the user
had to press F5.

Root cause: fetchTasks() runs while the task is still frozen, so
renderTasks() correctly skips the frozen task's detail panel. But after
unfreezeTask() in the finally block, no re-render was triggered.

The scan process already solved this by calling refreshSingleTask()
after unfreezeTask(). This fix applies the same principle directly in
unfreezeTask() — re-rendering from the existing tasks array (no extra
API call needed).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add `export-audit` CLI command for wallet transaction CSV export (#7700)

Wallet needs to export transactions as a CSV for auditing, with optional
date range filtering and fiat conversion.

## Changes

### `cli.rs`
- New `ExportAuditArgs` struct with: `--output-file`, `--start-date`,
`--end-date`, `--conversion-rate`, `--currency` (default: `USD`)
- New `ExportAudit(ExportAuditArgs)` variant in `CliCommands`
- `parse_utc_datetime` helper accepting RFC3339 and `YYYY-MM-DD` /
`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` formats

### `commands.rs`
- `ExportAudit` handler: fetches all completed transactions, filters by
date range, calls `write_audit_to_csv_file`
- `write_audit_to_csv_file`: emits CSV with columns matching the spec —
transaction type is `Deposit` (inbound/coinbase) or `Withdraw`
(outbound); transaction hash derived from first kernel hash; amounts in
XTM (Minotari); optional fiat column using supplied rate

### `wallet_modes.rs`
- Added `ExportAudit` arm to the exhaustive command-match test

## Example

```bash
minotari_console_wallet export-audit \
  --output-file audit.csv \
  --start-date 2021-01-01 \
  --end-date 2021-12-31 \
  --conversion-rate 3130.0 \
  --currency USD
```

Output CSV columns:
```
ID, Transaction Hash, Status, Transaction Type, DateTime (UTC),
From Address, To Address, Amount, AmountTicker, Amount (USD),
Txn Fee, FeeTicker, Fee (USD)
```

commit.message:
Decompiler fidelity: quick wins and medium-effort improvements (#277)

* feat(decompiler): improve C# emission fidelity

- Double/float literals: use round-trip format ("R") for shortest
  representation and append 'd' suffix with decimal point handling
- Nullable shorthand: rewrite Nullable<T> → T? in type names
- Checked arithmetic: emit checked(x + y) for *_ovf opcodes
- Checked conversions: emit checked((T)x) for conv.*_ovf opcodes
- Bare default: emit 'default' instead of 'default(T)' in initobj
- Null-coalescing: detect dup+brtrue+pop IL pattern, emit x ?? y
- ldtoken safety: emit comment for unresolved field/method tokens

Add 6 test fixture methods and 7 test assertions covering all changes.
All 168 tests pass (was 162).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add decompiler regression tests

* Fix conv.u2 char→ushort and harden null-coalescing pattern

- Conv_u2/Conv_ovf_u2: emit (ushort) instead of (char) — both compile
  to conv.u2 but ushort is the correct numeric default without type
  inference context
- Null-coalescing: guard against multiple stack slot assignments and
  document the S_in_0 invariant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add array initializer detection, enum constant names, and ldtoken fixes

Array initializers:
- Detect stelem(dup(newarr)) pattern and collapse into new T[] { ... }
- Handles sparse initialization (default for missing indices)

Enum constant resolution:
- Add ExpectedType annotation to ILAstExpression (set on call arguments)
- ILAstBuilder annotates call args with parameter types from signatures
- Emitter resolves enum field names for types in the current assembly
- BCL enums still show raw values (need cross-assembly metadata)

ldtoken field/method qualification:
- ResolveTokenName now includes declaring type (e.g., Type::Field)
- Fixes incorrect typeof() emission for field tokens (now emits comment)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Emit suffixed literals instead of casts for constant conversions

- Conv_i8 on ldc.i4: (long)3 → 3L
- Conv_u8 on ldc.i4: (ulong)3 → 3UL
- Conv_r4 on ldc.i4: (float)3 → 3.0f
- Conv_r8 on ldc.i4: (double)3 → 3.0d

Variable casts remain unchanged — only constant-to-constant folding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix decompiler token and conversion emission

- Fix dangling 'or' in StackSimulator ldtoken HandleKind pattern match
- Fix IsMethodMemberReference to use context.Reader (MetadataReader) instead
  of reader (ILReaderLite)
- Propagate ExpectedType through TryEmitInlinedReturn and TryEmitStoreReturn
  paths so conv.u2 emits (char) when method returns char
- Remove __methodref test fixture (C# 14 syntax not available in this SDK)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add no-amend commit policy to AGENT.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor ExpectedType from AST mutation to emission-context parameter

Pass expectedType as a parameter to EmitExpression instead of mutating
ILAstExpression.ExpectedType during emission. This prevents shared AST
nodes (e.g. from dup wrapping) from accumulating stale type annotations
across multiple emission sites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix invalid -1UL literal for negative conv.u8 constants

Negative int-to-ulong constants like ldc.i4.m1 + conv.u8 were emitting
-1UL which is invalid C# (unary minus can't apply to ulong). Now emits
unchecked((ulong)-1) for negative values, keeps NUL suffix for >= 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
